memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing for me to come, which means a lot given how much I value our group. But with the midterm tomorrow and my review only at 60%, skipping study time feels like a risky bet on future grades.

My close friends are missing, which is odd since I prioritize our group above all. With my stress already high and sleep debt mounting, this absence feels like a gap in my support system right when I need it most.
My message to Mina and Jae sets a clear boundary, prioritizing my long-term academic health over immediate social pressure despite the stress of missing them.
My study progress is solid, but letting the club opportunity slip away leaves me feeling isolated when I need my cohort's support most.

I apologized to Mina and Jae, yet my priority remains securing our bond; the exam is secondary to ensuring my cohort feels supported.
Mina's gratitude confirms our bond is strengthening, validating my choice to prioritize her feelings over the exam. This connection is exactly the foundation I need for the long term.
